title: Your path into Changemappers, {{name}}
intro: This journey is here to support orientation, not to hurry you. Mark a practice done only when it has actually happened. Reopen any step when it becomes relevant again.

pace.title: No deadline, no pressure
pace.body: The old 12-week sequence is now a rhythm you own. Some people finish a practice in a day, some return to it months later. Both are valid. The point is honest integration, not speed.

next.title: Next available step
next.complete: All practices are marked done. You can still revisit any step whenever your work changes.

completion.title: Journey complete
completion.subtitle: You have worked through all 12 practices. Keep integrating them at your own pace — the map awaits.

errors.saveFailed: Could not save this step. Please try again.

welcome.title: Welcome and orient
welcome.body: Enter gently. Look around the map, notice what is already moving, and let yourself learn the shape of the ecosystem before committing anywhere.
welcome.practice: Done when you have browsed the map or introduced yourself in a way that feels honest.
welcome.quote: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world; indeed, it is the only thing that ever has.
welcome.subject: Welcome to Changemappers. Your work starts here.
welcome.details: This is an orientation threshold, not a performance test. Let the map, channels, and people show you what is already alive before you decide where to contribute.
welcome.winWinWin: You win by entering at a sustainable pace, others win by meeting a clearer person, and the commons wins because attention goes where it can actually help.

inner_condition.title: Check your inner condition
inner_condition.body: Before acting outward, notice your capacity. Changemaking needs people who can sense exhaustion, resentment, excitement, and sustainable energy clearly.
inner_condition.practice: Done when you have written or spoken a real burnout and purpose check.
inner_condition.quote: The success of an intervention depends on the inner condition of the intervener.
inner_condition.subject: How are you, really? The burnout check.
inner_condition.details: Before changing systems, check the instrument you use to touch those systems: your own attention, body, motives, and capacity.
inner_condition.winWinWin: You win through self-honesty, collaborators win through steadier contact, and the wider system wins because exhausted urgency is not steering the work.

pitch.title: Structure your stand
pitch.body: Clarify what you see, what you are working toward, and how others can join without needing to decode your whole life story.
pitch.practice: Done when your profile or pitch has a usable version of your current stand.
pitch.quote: Be the change that you wish to see in the world.
pitch.subject: What do you stand for? Writing your pitch.
pitch.details: A good pitch is not a sales script. It is a compact invitation that makes your problem, vision, role, and invitation legible.
pitch.winWinWin: You win by clarifying your stand, potential allies win because the invitation is readable, and the ecosystem wins by reducing coordination friction.

regenerative_practice.title: Choose regenerative practice
regenerative_practice.body: Look at whether your methods replenish or extract from your body, your team, and the wider system.
regenerative_practice.practice: Done when you have named one practice you will keep, change, or stop.
regenerative_practice.quote: To love is not to reduce one's complexity.
regenerative_practice.subject: Tending to complexity and yourself.
regenerative_practice.details: Regenerative work asks whether your methods replenish life or quietly extract from bodies, teams, and places while claiming progress.
regenerative_practice.winWinWin: You win by refusing self-extraction, collaborators win through more humane methods, and the living system wins when complexity is tended rather than flattened.

failure_learning.title: Normalize learning from failure
failure_learning.body: Perfectionism blocks contact with reality. Share or privately document what broke, what it taught you, and what changes next.
failure_learning.practice: Done when one useful lesson from failure has been captured.
failure_learning.quote: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
failure_learning.subject: Fail fast, fail forward.
failure_learning.details: Failure is data when it is handled without shame. The point is not to celebrate damage, but to extract learning early enough to change course.
failure_learning.winWinWin: You win by turning shame into learning, peers win from reusable lessons, and the commons wins when experiments improve instead of hiding.

working_group.title: Find your people
working_group.body: Systemic work is too heavy alone. Look for a working group, initiative, circle, or task where your presence can be real and bounded.
working_group.practice: Done when you have joined, started, or deliberately declined a concrete group.
working_group.quote: If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
working_group.subject: You cannot do this alone.
working_group.details: Systemic change is too heavy to carry alone. Look for a community of practice, transformative circle, or action research group where contribution and learning can happen together.
working_group.winWinWin: You win through support and focus, the group wins from your bounded contribution, and the system wins when isolated effort becomes shared capacity.

mutual_aid.title: Practice mutual aid
mutual_aid.body: A healthy commons includes giving and receiving. Asking clearly is part of participation, not a weakness.
mutual_aid.practice: Done when you have posted, drafted, or directly made one clear ask.
mutual_aid.quote: If you have come here to help me, you are wasting your time. But if your liberation is bound up with mine, then let us work together.
mutual_aid.subject: Moving from charity to solidarity
mutual_aid.details: A healthy commons includes giving and receiving. Asking clearly is part of participation, not a weakness.
mutual_aid.winWinWin: Use this step to choose a next move that protects your capacity, offers clear value to collaborators, and strengthens the wider commons.

energy_alignment.title: Align with your energy
energy_alignment.body: Review your commitments. Notice which tasks give energy, which drain it, and where you are filling gaps from guilt.
energy_alignment.practice: Done when one commitment has been adjusted toward a more sustainable role.
energy_alignment.quote: Do not ask what the world needs. Ask what makes you come alive, and go do it.
energy_alignment.subject: Stop doing work you hate.
energy_alignment.details: Review your commitments. Notice which tasks give energy, which drain it, and where you are filling gaps from guilt.
energy_alignment.winWinWin: Use this step to choose a next move that protects your capacity, offers clear value to collaborators, and strengthens the wider commons.

systems_connection.title: See across systems
systems_connection.body: Find a project outside your usual field and look for intersections. Climate, housing, care, governance, and learning often meet in unexpected places.
systems_connection.practice: Done when you have explored or contacted one project beyond your familiar area.
systems_connection.quote: The earth is but one country, and mankind its citizens.
systems_connection.subject: Seeing the whole board.
systems_connection.details: Find a project outside your usual field and look for intersections. Climate, housing, care, governance, and learning often meet in unexpected places.
systems_connection.winWinWin: Use this step to choose a next move that protects your capacity, offers clear value to collaborators, and strengthens the wider commons.

friction_grace.title: Hold friction with grace
friction_grace.body: Conflict is part of collective work. Practice separating impact from intention and keeping critique focused on the idea or process.
friction_grace.practice: Done when you have used or prepared one healthier conflict move.
friction_grace.quote: Do to others as you would have them do to you.
friction_grace.subject: When the community disagrees.
friction_grace.details: Conflict is part of collective work. Practice separating impact from intention and keeping critique focused on the idea or process.
friction_grace.winWinWin: Use this step to choose a next move that protects your capacity, offers clear value to collaborators, and strengthens the wider commons.

boundaries.title: Protect your boundaries
boundaries.body: Sustainable contribution needs limits. Review your notifications, visibility, pace, and what kind of contact is welcome.
boundaries.practice: Done when one boundary or setting reflects your real capacity.
boundaries.quote: Caring for myself is not self-indulgence, it is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare.
boundaries.subject: Protecting your most valuable asset.
boundaries.details: Sustainable contribution needs limits. Review your notifications, visibility, pace, and what kind of contact is welcome.
boundaries.winWinWin: Use this step to choose a next move that protects your capacity, offers clear value to collaborators, and strengthens the wider commons.

cycle_of_joy.title: Return to the cycle of joy
cycle_of_joy.body: The work does not end, so joy cannot be postponed until completion. Notice where service, respect, and aliveness meet.
cycle_of_joy.practice: Done when you have marked what makes this work worth continuing.
cycle_of_joy.quote: I slept and dreamt that life was joy. I awoke and saw that life was service. I acted and behold, service was joy.
cycle_of_joy.subject: The marathon continues.
cycle_of_joy.details: The work does not end, so joy cannot be postponed until completion. Notice where service, respect, and aliveness meet.
cycle_of_joy.winWinWin: Use this step to choose a next move that protects your capacity, offers clear value to collaborators, and strengthens the wider commons.
